agent_output: {"summary":{"verdict":"BLOCK","one_liner":"Critical security flaws: broken hash algorithm, timing-attack-vulnerable comparison, and no token expiration enforcement make these tokens trivially forgeable and exploitable in production."},"critical_findings":[{"title":"MD5 is cryptographically broken — tokens are forgeable","risk":"MD5 has known collision and preimage attacks. An attacker who observes any token can brute-force the secretKey or forge tokens for arbitrary partnerIds. MD5 is explicitly prohibited by NIST, PCI-DSS, and OWASP for any security-sensitive use.","severity":"Critical","location":"generatePartnerApiToken() and validatePartnerApiToken() — `crypto.createHash('md5')`","reproduction":"Use hashcat or any MD5 cracking tool against a captured token. The payload structure `partnerId:timestamp` is predictable, reducing the search space to the secretKey alone.","fix":"Replace with HMAC-SHA256: `crypto.createHmac('sha256', secretKey).update(payload).digest('hex')`. HMAC is the correct primitive here — it's designed for keyed message authentication, unlike a bare hash where key concatenation is vulnerable to length-extension attacks.","time_to_fix":"15 minutes","acceptance_criteria_affected":"Token security, partner trust, compliance (PCI/SOC2)"},{"title":"Timing attack on token comparison leaks valid tokens","risk":"`token === expectedToken` uses JavaScript's default string comparison, which short-circuits on the first mismatched character. An attacker can measure response times across thousands of requests to determine the correct token one character at a time.","severity":"Critical","location":"validatePartnerApiToken() — `return token === expectedToken`","reproduction":"Send validation requests with tokens that share progressively longer prefixes with the real token. Measure response latency differences (typically 10-50ns per character, amplifiable with statistical analysis over ~10k requests).","fix":"Use `crypto.timingSafeEqual(Buffer.from(token, 'hex'), Buffer.from(expectedToken, 'hex'))`. This performs constant-time comparison regardless of where characters differ.","time_to_fix":"5 minutes","acceptance_criteria_affected":"Token validation security"},{"title":"No token expiration enforcement — 30-day lifetime is unenforced","risk":"validatePartnerApiToken accepts a timestamp parameter but never checks whether the token has expired. Any token ever issued remains valid forever. If a partner is offboarded or a token is leaked, there is no expiration boundary. The stated 30-day lifetime is a lie.","severity":"Critical","location":"validatePartnerApiToken() — no expiration check on `timestamp`","reproduction":"Generate a token today. Call validatePartnerApiToken with the original timestamp 6 months from now. It returns true.","fix":"Add expiration check at the top of validatePartnerApiToken: `const TOKEN_TTL_MS = 30 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000; if (Date.now() - timestamp > TOKEN_TTL_MS) return false;`. Also: generatePartnerApiToken should return the timestamp alongside the token so callers can store it for validation.","time_to_fix":"10 minutes","acceptance_criteria_affected":"Token lifecycle management, partner offboarding safety"}],"important_issues":[{"title":"Length-extension attack on hash(payload + secret) construction","description":"Even after upgrading from MD5, the pattern `hash(message + secret)` is vulnerable to length-extension attacks with SHA-256 and other Merkle-Damgård hashes. An attacker who knows a valid token can append data and compute a valid hash without knowing the secret. This is exactly why HMAC exists — it applies the key in an inner and outer pass that prevents this class of attack. Switching to HMAC (as recommended in Critical #1) eliminates this entirely.","location":"Both functions — payload + secretKey concatenation pattern"},{"title":"generatePartnerApiToken returns only the token, not the timestamp","description":"The timestamp used during generation is lost. The caller has no way to pass the correct timestamp to validatePartnerApiToken later. This means validation is impossible without storing the timestamp externally — but the API doesn't surface it. The function should return `{ token, timestamp }` or encode the timestamp into the token itself.","location":"generatePartnerApiToken() return value"},{"title":"No input validation on partnerId or secretKey","description":"Empty string, null prototype injection, or strings containing the delimiter ':' in partnerId could cause token collisions between different partners. For example, partnerId 'a:123' with timestamp 456 produces the same payload as partnerId 'a' with timestamp '123:456'. Add validation that partnerId matches an expected format (e.g., alphanumeric).","location":"Both functions — no validation on inputs"}],"suggestions":["Consider using a standard token format like JWT (jsonwebtoken library) which handles signing, expiration, and payload encoding correctly out of the box — eliminates all three critical findings","Add a token revocation mechanism (database blocklist or Redis set) for immediate invalidation when a partner is offboarded","Rotate secretKey per-partner rather than sharing a global key — limits blast radius if one partner's token is compromised","Add rate limiting on the validation endpoint to make timing attacks and brute-force attacks impractical"],"production_readiness":{"deployment_risks":"Tokens generated with the current MD5 scheme will be incompatible after switching to HMAC-SHA256. All existing partner tokens must be re-issued during migration. Coordinate with partners before deploying.","rollback_capability":"Rolling back after deploying the fix would re-expose the MD5 vulnerability. Plan a one-way migration: issue new HMAC tokens, keep a time-limited fallback validator for old tokens during a grace period, then remove MD5 validation entirely.","backward_compatibility":"Breaking change — all existing tokens become invalid after hash algorithm change. Requires a migration strategy with partner communication.","monitoring_coverage":"Add logging for: expired token rejections, validation failures (potential attack signal), and token generation events. Alert on validation failure rate spikes.","breaking_changes":{"api_breaking":true,"db_destructive":false,"shared_library":false,"auth_security":true,"data_pipeline":false,"config_changes":false,"details":["Hash algorithm change invalidates all existing partner tokens","Adding expiration enforcement may reject currently-accepted tokens that are past 30 days"]},"safe_to_deploy":false,"rollback_plan":"Do not deploy current code. Fix all three critical issues first, then deploy with a dual-validation grace period: accept both old MD5 tokens and new HMAC tokens for 30 days, then cut over entirely."},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":97,"high_confidence_areas":["MD5 weakness is universally accepted — not debatable","Timing attack via === on secrets is a textbook vulnerability","Missing expiration check is verifiable by reading the code — there is no check"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether existing tokens are already deployed to partners (affects migration urgency)"],"assumptions":["secretKey has sufficient entropy (not validated in this review)","This code runs server-side, not in a browser"]}}
